clusters.title: United Flight Strikes Pole and Truck Near Newark Airport
clusters.summary: A United Airlines flight struck a light pole and a delivery truck while approaching Newark Liberty International Airport, resulting in damage to the truck and injuries to the driver. The incident occurred on the New Jersey Turnpike, raising concerns about safety protocols during landing. Authorities are investigating the circumstances surrounding the collision.

items.title: Watch: Moment United Airlines flight strikes vehicle during landing
items.description: Flight 169 from Venice, Italy, landed safely at Newark Airport with no reported injuries among the 231 passengers and crew. The lorry driver sustained minor injuries.

items.title: Driver injured after truck was struck by United plane landing at Newark airport
items.description:
Delivery truck driver emerged with minor injuries after underside of jet hit tractor trailer in New Jersey on Sunday

The driver of a delivery truck emerged with only minor injuries after his tractor trailer was struck by a passenger jet landing in New Jersey’s busy Newark airport on Sunday afternoon, officials said.

New Jersey state police said a landing tire and the underside of a United Airlines plane arriving from Venice, Italy, hit the truck in question. The Boeing 767 aircraft also clipped a light pole, which in turn struck a Jeep.

Continue reading...

items.description: New video captures the harrowing moment a United Airlines flight hit a light pole and tractor trailer during its descent into Newark Liberty airport.

clusters.title: Trump Announces US Navy Will Escort Ships in Strait of Hormuz
clusters.summary: President Donald Trump has declared that the US Navy will assist in guiding stranded vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping route. This announcement comes amid rising tensions with Iran, which has issued warnings against foreign military presence in the area, stating that such actions could lead to attacks. The shipping industry is expressing concerns about safety in the region following these developments.

items.title: Shipping firms question safety in strait of Hormuz despite Trump plan
items.description:
President says US navy will ‘guide’ stranded ships out of waterway but report says warship was hit by Iran

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

The world’s shipping industry has questioned if vessels will be able to travel safely to and from the Gulf after Donald Trump announced his latest plan to open the strait of Hormuz.

The US president wrote on Monday that the navy would “guide” stranded ships out of the waterway, writing on his social media site Truth Social that the operation, “Project Freedom”, would be a humanitarian gesture “on behalf of the United States, Middle Eastern Countries but, in particular, the Country of Iran”.

Continue reading...

items.description: President Donald Trump has announced that the United States military will help guide stranded ships out of the Strait of Hormuz. clusters.title: Three Dead in Suspected Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship
clusters.summary: Three individuals have died and three others are ill following a suspected hantavirus outbreak aboard the MV Hondius, a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean. Health officials, including the World Health Organization, have stated that the risk to the general public remains low despite the incident. Investigations are ongoing to confirm the cause of the illnesses and to ensure the safety of passengers and crew.

items.title: Three dead in suspected virus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
items.description: The operator of the MV Hondius ship says a Dutch husband and wife, as well as a German national, have died but the cause has not yet been established.

items.title: What to Know About the Suspected Hantavirus Outbreak on an Atlantic Cruise Ship
items.description: Three passengers of the MV Hondius died after showing symptoms of the rare disease. Health authorities confirmed one case and are investigating five others.

items.title: What is hantavirus, suspected in deaths of three people on cruise ship?

items.description: Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship Leaves 3 Dead, WHO Says  Bloomberg.com

items.title: Three passengers dead after suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
items.description:
Dutch husband and wife and third unidentified person reported to have died, with three further people taken ill

  • What is hantavirus, the infection thought to have killed three on cruise ship?

A suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus infection on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean killed three people – including an elderly married couple – and sickened at least three others, the World Health Organisation (WHO) and South Africa’s health department said on Sunday.

The WHO said an investigation was under way but that at least one case of hantavirus had been confirmed. One of the patients was in intensive care in a South African hospital, the UN’s health agency said in a statement to the Associated Press, and the WHO was working with authorities to evacuate two others with symptoms from the ship.

Continue reading...

items.title: What Is Hantavirus, Which Is Linked to the Deaths of 3 People Aboard a Cruise Ship?
items.description: Three people aboard a cruise ship sailing the Atlantic Ocean have died from what health officials suspect were hantavirus infections.

clusters.title: Trump Declares End to Hostilities with Iran Amid War Powers Deadline
clusters.summary: President Trump informed Congress that hostilities with Iran have 'terminated,' coinciding with the 60-day deadline set under the War Powers Resolution. This declaration comes as tensions in the region have been ongoing, and the statement raises questions about the administration's future military engagements. The announcement is significant as it reflects the administration's stance on the conflict and its legal obligations to Congress.

clusters.title: Supreme Court Petitioned to Restore Mail Access for Abortion Pill
clusters.summary: The manufacturer of the abortion pill Mifepristone has petitioned the Supreme Court to restore access to the medication by mail. This request comes in response to recent legal challenges that have imposed restrictions on mail-order prescriptions. The outcome could significantly impact access to abortion services across the country.

clusters.summary: The United States is set to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany, a move announced by President Trump, who indicated that further reductions may follow. clusters.title: Marco Rubio to Meet Pope Leo XIV Amid Tensions with Vatican
clusters.summary: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio is scheduled to travel to Rome this week for a meeting with Pope Leo XIV. This visit comes as the Trump administration seeks to address strained relations with the Vatican, particularly regarding differing views on Iran. The meeting is seen as a potential step towards mending diplomatic ties between the U.S. and the Holy See.

The state department said in a statement that the meeting will be “to advance bilateral relations with Italy and the Vatican”.

clusters.title: Supreme Court Ruling Limits Voting Rights Act, Sparks Redistricting
clusters.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court has issued a ruling that restricts the application of the Voting Rights Act, prompting states to begin redistricting efforts. This decision has been met with strong reactions, including claims of a significant setback for democracy. The ruling is expected to influence electoral maps and voting access across the country.

clusters.title: EU Leaders Meet in Armenia Amid Shifting Regional Alliances
clusters.summary: European leaders convened in Armenia for two significant summits focused on security, trade, and the ongoing war in Ukraine. This gathering marks a notable shift for Armenia, traditionally seen as a close ally of Russia, as it seeks to strengthen ties with the European Union. The discussions highlight the evolving geopolitical landscape in the region and the implications for Armenia's foreign policy.

clusters.title: Yankees Radio Announcer John Sterling Passes Away at 87
clusters.summary: John Sterling, the iconic radio voice of the New York Yankees, has died at the age of 87. Sterling, who began announcing Yankees games in 1989, was renowned for his unique home run calls and had a record of over 5,600 games with the team. His passing marks the end of an era for Yankees fans and sports broadcasting.

items.title: John Sterling, the Yankees’ Most Enduring Announcer, Dies at 87
items.description: He called over 5,600 games, more than any other announcer for the team, and was known for his personalized, slightly wacky home run calls.

clusters.title: Berkshire Hathaway's New CEO Greg Abel Leads First Annual Meeting

clusters.title: F1 Plans Return to V8 Engines by 2030 or 2031
clusters.summary: Formula One is set to reintroduce traditional V8 engines by 2030, with a possibility of implementation by 2031, as announced by FIA President Mohammed Ben Sulayem during the Miami Grand Prix. This decision comes amid discussions about the future of the sport's engine regulations and the effectiveness of current hybrid technologies. The move aims to balance performance with sustainability in the evolving landscape of motorsport.

clusters.title: Ukrainian Drone Strikes Moscow High-Rise Ahead of Victory Day
clusters.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack targeted a high-rise building in Moscow, breaching the city's air defenses just days before the annual Victory Day military parade. This incident highlights the ongoing conflict between Ukraine and Russia, as Ukraine continues to extend its long-range strike capabilities. The Kremlin is reportedly scaling back the parade in light of security concerns following the attack.

items.description: Air defenses in the Russian capital were breached as Ukraine expands long-range strikes.

clusters.title: Two US Service Members Missing in Morocco After Exercises
clusters.summary: Two US service members have been reported missing in southwestern Morocco following their participation in annual multinational military exercises. A search and rescue operation has been initiated by the United States Africa Command in the area near the city of Tan Tan. The incident raises concerns about the safety of personnel involved in international military collaborations.

items.title: 2 US service members missing after military exercises in Morocco
items.description: They taking part in an annual multinational military exercises in Morocco.

items.title: Two US service members missing after military exercises in Morocco
items.description:
Search and rescue operation launched after service members reported missing near south-western city of Tan Tan

Two US service members are missing in south-western Morocco after taking part in annual multinational military exercises in the North African country, the United States Africa Command (Africom) said on Sunday.

The US, Morocco and other countries participating in the African Lion exercise have launched a search and rescue operation, Africom said.

Continue reading...
